pretax.description: Whether the benefit is deducted before tax calculations, thus reducing one’s taxable income

posttax.description: Whether the benefit is deducted after tax calculations.

imputed.description: Whether the benefit is considered imputed income.

healthcare.description: Whether the benefit is healthcare related.

retirement.description: Whether the benefit is associated with retirement planning.

yearly_limit.description: Whether the benefit has a government mandated yearly limit. If the benefit has a government mandated yearly limit, employees cannot be added to more than one benefit of this type.

category.description: Category where the benefit belongs to.

writable_by_application.description: Whether this benefit can be written (created, updated, or destroyed). Returns true if the benefit type is permitted for the application, false otherwise.

Example.name: Medical Insurance
Example.description: Deductions and contributions for Medical Insurance

value.name: Medical Insurance
value.description: Deductions and contributions for Medical Insurance

value.name: Dental Insurance
value.description: Deductions and contributions for Dental Insurance

value.name: Vision Insurance
value.description: Deductions and contributions for Vision Insurance

value.name: Health Savings Account
value.description: Health Savings Accounts (HSA) allow employees to be reimbursed for qualified medical expenses. Contributions are pre-tax and lower the total amount of tax paid by employees and the employer. Employers may also make tax-free contributions to employees' HSA. Remaining balances are carried over in next year.

value.name: Health FSA
value.description: Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA) allow employees to be reimbursed for qualified medical expenses. Contributions are pre-tax and lower the total amount of tax paid by employees and the employer. Employers may also make tax-free contributions to employees' FSA. Remaining balances are not carried over in next year.

value.name: Dependent Care FSA
value.description: Dependent Care FSA reimburses employees for expenses to care for dependents while the employee is at work (e.g. Daycares). Contributions are pre-tax and lower the total amount of tax paid by employees and the employer. Employers may also make tax-free contributions to employee FSA. Remaining balances are not carried over to the next year. Single parents or Married couples filing a joint return can elect up to $5000 per year. Married couples filing separate returns are limited to $2500 elections each.

value.name: SIMPLE IRA
value.description: The SIMPLE IRA is a tax-deferred retirement savings plan for employees. It is often used by small businesses as an alternative to 401(k) due to its relatively low operating cost. Employers are required to contribute a specific percentage to an employee's SIMPLE IRA.

value.name: Roth 401(k)
value.description: Roth 401(k) is an after-tax savings plan for employees. The standard maximum is $18,000, or $24,000 for employees over 50 years old.

value.name: Roth 403(b)
value.description: Roth 403(b) is an after-tax savings plan for certain clerics, employees of public schools, and employees of other types of tax-exempt organizations. Contributions made by employees are taxable for federal and state withholding. Often, employers contribute additional pre-tax dollars to the employee’s Roth account to encourage saving for retirement.

value.name: 401(k)
value.description: 401(k) is tax-deferred retirement savings plan for employees. The standard maximum is $18,000, or $24,000 for employees over 50 years old.

value.name: 403(b)
value.description: 403(b) is tax-deferred retirement savings plan for certain clerics, employees of public schools, and employees of other types of tax-exempt organizations.

value.name: SEP-IRA
value.description: A SEP-IRA is a pre-tax retirement savings plan where only the employer contributes. It is often used by small businesses as an alternative to 401(k) due to its relatively low operating cost. Employers are required to contribute the same percentage to all enrolled employees, with a maximum contribution of 25% of the employee’s compensation.

value.name: SARSEP
value.description: A SARSEP is a pre-tax retirement savings plan used by small businesses as an alternative to 401(k) due to its relatively low operating cost. While new SARSEP plans are not available, there are still some companies that are grandfathered into the plan. Employers are required to contribute the same percentage to all enrolled employees, with a maximum contribution of 25% of the employee’s compensation.

value.name: Group-Term Life Insurance
value.description: Group-Term Life Insurance for coverage in excess of $50,000 per employee is a taxable fringe benefit. See IRS Publication 15-B to determine the dollar value of the excess coverage.

value.name: Commuter Benefits (pre-tax)
value.description: Tax-free commuter benefits for transit, vanpooling, bicycling, and work-related parking costs. The annual maximum contribution for this pre-tax benefit is in the IRS publication 15-B.

value.name: Personal Use of Company Car
value.description: When an employee uses a company-owned car for personal matters, it is considered taxable benefit provided in-kind.

value.name: 529 College Savings
value.description: 529 College Savings is an after-tax savings plan for employees designed to encourage saving for future college costs. This benefit should be reported as a taxable benefit and will therefore be taxed.

value.name: Student Loan Repayment
value.description: Student Loan Repayment is an after-tax savings plan for employees to pay towards their outstanding student loans. An employee can choose to set aside after-tax dollars towards this benefit. These benefits should be reported as a taxable benefit and will therefore be taxed.

value.name: Short Term Disability (post-tax)
value.description: Third Party Disability or Third Party Leave are policies offered by employers that pay an employee for a specific life event (maternity leave, injury). All payments made to employees come from a third-party, such as an insurer. For more information on the taxation of these plans, please refer to publication 15-A for more details.

value.name: Long Term Disability (post-tax)
value.description: Third Party Disability or Third Party Leave are policies offered by employers that pay an employee for a specific life event (maternity leave, injury). All payments made to employees come from a third-party, such as an insurer. For more information on the taxation of these plans, please refer to publication 15-A for more details.

value.name: Short Term Disability (pre-tax)
value.description: Third Party Disability or Third Party Leave are policies offered by employers that pay an employee for a specific life event (maternity leave, injury). All payments made to employees come from a third-party, such as an insurer. For more information on the taxation of these plans, please refer to publication 15-A for more details.

value.name: Long Term Disability (pre-tax)
value.description: Third Party Disability or Third Party Leave are policies offered by employers that pay an employee for a specific life event (maternity leave, injury). All payments made to employees come from a third-party, such as an insurer. For more information on the taxation of these plans, please refer to publication 15-A for more details.

value.name: Voluntary Short Term Disability (post-tax)
value.description: Third Party Disability or Third Party Leave are policies offered by employers that pay an employee for a specific life event (maternity leave, injury). All payments made to employees come from a third-party, such as an insurer. For more information on the taxation of these plans, please refer to publication 15-A for more details.

value.name: Voluntary Long Term Disability (post-tax)
value.description: Third Party Disability or Third Party Leave are policies offered by employers that pay an employee for a specific life event (maternity leave, injury). All payments made to employees come from a third-party, such as an insurer. For more information on the taxation of these plans, please refer to publication 15-A for more details.

value.name: Voluntary Life (post-tax)
value.description: Third Party Disability or Third Party Leave are policies offered by employers that pay an employee for a specific life event (maternity leave, injury). All payments made to employees come from a third-party, such as an insurer. For more information on the taxation of these plans, please refer to publication 15-A for more details.

value.name: Commuter Parking
value.description: Tax-free commuter benefits allow employees to reduce their monthly commuting expenses for transit, carpooling, bicycling, and work-related parking costs. Please note that there is an annual maximum for this pre-tax benefit. The maximum dollar amount is found in IRS Publication 15-B.

value.name: Commuter Transit
value.description: Tax-free commuter benefits allow employees to reduce their monthly commuting expenses for transit, carpooling, bicycling, and work-related parking costs. Please note that there is an annual maximum for this pre-tax benefit. The maximum dollar amount is found in IRS Publication 15-B.

value.name: Other (taxable)
value.description: Other taxable benefit

value.name: Cell Phone (taxable)
value.description: Employer-sponsored benefits like this are called fringe benefits, and they don’t get special tax treatment—they’ll be reported as taxable wages on your employees’ paystubs.

value.name: Gym & Fitness (taxable)
value.description: Employer-sponsored benefits like this are called fringe benefits, and they don’t get special tax treatment—they’ll be reported as taxable wages on your employees’ paystubs.

value.name: Housing (taxable)
value.description: Employer-sponsored benefits like this are called fringe benefits, and they don’t get special tax treatment—they’ll be reported as taxable wages on your employees’ paystubs.

value.name: Wellness (taxable)
value.description: Employer-sponsored benefits like this are called fringe benefits, and they don’t get special tax treatment—they’ll be reported as taxable wages on your employees’ paystubs.
